Operation 'deleteVoice' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary and description are identical and overly simplistic. They lack critical context about what 'delete' means (permanent removal), authentication requirements, side effects (references becoming invalid), and what response to expect. Added specificity about ID-based deletion, authentication, permanence, and return behavior to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Delete a voice by ID
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a voice resource by its ID. Requires authentication via bearer token. Once deleted, the voice cannot be recovered and any references to it may become invalid. Returns a success response on completion.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are identical and overly simplistic. They lack critical context about what 'delete' means (permanent removal), authentication requirements, side effects (references becoming invalid), and what response to expect. Added specificity about ID-based deletion, authentication, permanence, and return behavior to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Generate a voice preview sample
data.description_suggestion: Generates a preview audio sample of a voice with specified text content. Useful for evaluating voice characteristics before using in production workflows. Requires authentication.

data.summary_suggestion: Delete an avatar by ID
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes an avatar resource identified by its ID. This operation removes the avatar from the system and cannot be undone. Requires authentication and the user must have permission to delete the specified avatar. Returns a success response with no content on successful deletion.
